dial.summary: Net score holds at +1 (3 supportive: Fed balance sheet flat at $6.72T, BTC funding rates ~+0.01% subdued, stablecoin dominance ~13.6% on 30-day downtrend; 3 neutral: Global M2 ~$101.7T flat, ISM 53.3% expanding but falling, 2s10s +0.35%; 2 headwinds: DXY 100.97, real yields 2.28%) as BTC recovered to ~$64,154 (+2.2%) from yesterday's dip with BTC.D firm at 56.3%—the June CPI print on July 14 (alongside Fed Chair Warsh's Congressional testimony) is the key near-term catalyst that could flip both headwinds to neutral if inflation surprises to the downside.

indicators.name: Global Liquidity (Global M2)
indicators.note: 4-bank aggregate (US/ECB/BOJ/PBOC) at $100.86T through March 2026 (MacroMicro); US M2 at record $23.05T in May 2026 (H.6 release), with smaller central banks and cross-currency effects bringing the broader global estimate to ~$101.7T; DXY at ~101.0 modestly suppresses the USD-denominated value of foreign M2 relative to a weaker-dollar scenario; 4-week trend flat—US M2 growth has been gradual and the global aggregate changes slowly with data lagging 6–8 weeks; status neutral (4-week trend not clearly and consistently up to qualify as supportive; YoY growth is positive, not contracting).

indicators.name: ISM Manufacturing PMI
indicators.note: June 2026 ISM Manufacturing PMI confirmed at 53.3%, released July 1—sixth consecutive month of expansion but 0.7pp below May's 54.0%; new orders 56.0% (-0.8pp), production 52.2% (-2.1pp), employment 49.7% (+1.1pp, still contracting), prices 73.0% (-9.1pp, largest single-month drop since July 2022, signaling meaningful pipeline disinflation); no July 2026 data yet—next ISM Manufacturing PMI for July releases August 3; 4-week trend down (May 54.0% → June 53.3%); status neutral (above 50 but falling—supportive requires both above 50 AND rising).

indicators.name: DXY (US Dollar Index)
indicators.note: ICE DXY closed at ~100.97 on July 10, 2026 (up marginally from 100.92 on July 9); DXY broke above 100 in mid-June following the June 16–17 FOMC hold and Kevin Warsh hawkish dot-plot shift and has held in the 100.5–101.4 range since; 4-week trend up from approximately 100.7 on June 11 (ECB hike day) to 100.97 today (+0.27 points over ~4 weeks); status headwind (ICE DXY above the 100 neutral-band ceiling); June CPI on July 14 is the primary near-term catalyst—a soft print combined with any Warsh dovish tilt at his Congressional testimony that day could push DXY below 100 and flip status to neutral.

indicators.name: Real Yields (US10Y - CPI)
indicators.note: 10-year TIPS real yield (DFII10) at 2.28% on July 10, 2026, down 0.01pp from the prior session; 4-week trend up—yield was approximately 2.14% around June 11 and has risen ~0.14pp over the past month as the June FOMC hold and Warsh higher-for-longer messaging anchored nominal 10-year yields above 4.5%; status headwind (above 2% and rising on 4-week basis; supportive requires falling or below 1%); June CPI on July 14 is the pivotal catalyst—a soft print would compress breakevens and reduce real yields toward the neutral zone.

indicators.name: 2s10s Yield Curve
indicators.note: 10-year Treasury at 4.56% and 2-year at 4.21% as of July 10, 2026—spread +0.35% (35bps); 4-week trend flat: spread was approximately +0.37–0.40% in early June (pre-FOMC range), now +0.35%, a change of approximately -2 to -5bps within the <5bps flat threshold; status neutral (positive spread well clear of the -50bps deep-inversion headwind threshold, but not steepening—supportive requires positive AND steepening); next FOMC decision July 28–29 (no Summary of Economic Projections).

indicators.name: Fed Balance Sheet
indicators.note: H.4.1 balance sheet for week ended July 2, 2026 (released July 9 at 4:30 PM ET): total assets $6,724B ($6.72T) comprising Treasuries $4,492B, MBS $1,948B, other $284B; essentially unchanged from the prior 4-week period as QT proceeds at a minimal pace (~$1–2B/week in passive runoff); next H.4.1 release July 16 at 4:30 PM ET covering week ended July 9; 4-week trend flat; status supportive (holding flat—no acceleration of QT, no aggressive reserve drain).

indicators.name: BTC Funding Rates (perps avg)
indicators.note: BTC perpetual funding rate near +0.01% per 8-hour interval (Convex aggregate, late June–early July 2026); BTC recovered to ~$64,154 on July 11 (+2.2% from $62,756 on July 10) with BTC.D at 56.3%—the intraday bounce appears driven by organic spot demand rather than leveraged re-accumulation, consistent with the subdued funding environment; status supportive (well below the 0.04% overheating threshold; near-zero funding during BTC's recovery is a healthy sign for the accumulation thesis ahead of the July 14 CPI catalyst).

indicators.name: Stablecoin Dominance
indicators.note: Stablecoin market cap ~$310B against total crypto market cap ~$2.28T as of July 11, 2026 (CoinGecko: ~13.6%); 30-day trend down from approximately 14.3–14.5% in early June to ~13.6%, indicating net capital rotation into the crypto ecosystem over the past month; BTC recovering to $64,154 with BTC.D firm at 56.3% while stablecoin dominance trends lower is consistent with broad crypto accumulation; status supportive (30-day downtrend signals ongoing capital rotation into risk assets).

upcoming.title: CPI Inflation Report (June 2026)
upcoming.note: BLS Consumer Price Index for June 2026 at 8:30 AM ET—highest-impact event of the 14-day window and the last major inflation print before the July 28–29 FOMC (no dot plot); the ISM Manufacturing prices sub-index collapsed 9.1pp in June to 73.0%—the largest single-month drop since July 2022—pointing to meaningful pipeline disinflation that should show in core goods CPI; a soft CPI print could flip both DXY (100.97) and real yields (2.28%) from headwind to neutral, turning the dashboard score solidly positive for BTC; also on July 14 at 10:00 AM ET: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh appears before the House Financial Services Committee—the combination of CPI data and Chair Warsh testimony makes this the most market-moving day of the current window.

upcoming.title: PPI (Producer Price Index, June 2026)
upcoming.note: BLS Producer Price Index for June 2026 at 8:30 AM ET—upstream inflation gauge released one day after CPI; the 9.1pp collapse in ISM Manufacturing prices points to material final-goods PPI relief on domestic supply chains; soft back-to-back CPI and PPI prints would confirm the disinflationary pipeline is intact and reinforce FOMC confidence for a July 28–29 hold; Empire State Manufacturing Survey (July 2026) also at 8:30 AM ET—first regional factory read of Q3 and an early signal ahead of the August 3 national ISM Manufacturing release.

upcoming.title: Fed Beige Book (July 2026)
upcoming.note: Federal Reserve Beige Book released at 4:15 PM ET—anecdotal economic conditions from all 12 Fed districts, published two weeks before the July 28–29 FOMC meeting (no dot plot); will capture early-Q3 business sentiment on labor costs, consumer spending, tariff pass-through, and post-ISM-prices disinflation; a tone of softening demand and easing price pressures supports a hold at 5.25–5.50%, while persistent services inflation or energy re-acceleration language would reinforce the Warsh hawkish tilt and strengthen the case for a future hike.

upcoming.title: Retail Sales (June 2026)
upcoming.note: Census Bureau Advance Monthly Retail Trade for June 2026 at 8:30 AM ET—key test of consumer spending health; real yields at 2.28% and the 10-year at ~4.56% continue to weigh on big-ticket discretionary spending; a solid print reinforces the soft-landing narrative ahead of the July 28–29 FOMC; Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Survey (July 2026) also at 8:30 AM ET—second early-Q3 regional factory read alongside last week's Empire State; H.4.1 Fed balance sheet (week ended July 9) releases at 4:30 PM ET and will confirm whether QT pace remained minimal.

upcoming.title: Initial Jobless Claims (week ended July 12, 2026)
upcoming.note: Weekly UI claims at 8:30 AM ET (Thursday), same morning as retail sales; two consecutive weeks above 240K would mark the first concrete signal of broad labor deterioration and reinforce rate-cut expectations ahead of the July 28–29 FOMC; the paired labor-and-spending release on July 16 provides the clearest simultaneous read on early-Q3 consumer and labor health before the FOMC blackout period (beginning July 18) limits further Fed commentary.

upcoming.title: Housing Starts & Building Permits / Industrial Production / UMich Sentiment (June–July 2026)
upcoming.note: Census Bureau new residential construction for June at 8:30 AM ET—housing demand signal in the still-elevated real-yield environment (2.28%); Federal Reserve Industrial Production & Capacity Utilization (June) at 9:15 AM ET cross-checks the ISM Manufacturing expansion (53.3%, sixth consecutive month above 50); University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment preliminary (July) at 10:00 AM ET—watch the 1-year and 5-year inflation expectations components, which carry outsized weight for the Warsh Fed's reaction function; these are the last significant releases before the FOMC blackout begins July 18.
